personas.name: Cost-conscious explorer
personas.description: A user interested in improving their mental health but frustrated by limited access due to paywalls.
goals: Access meaningful mental health insights for free
Evaluate app value before committing to payment
pain_points: Most useful features are locked behind a subscription
Feels misled after initial free experience
context: Trying the app for the first time
Exploring mental health tools on a budget
constraints: Limited willingness or ability to pay

personas.name: Frustrated reliability-seeker
personas.description: A long-term user who relies on the app for consistency but is hampered by technical bugs and instability.
goals: Maintain a consistent daily logging habit
Receive timely notifications for mood check-ins
pain_points: App crashes or freezes after updates
Notifications failing to trigger, leading to missed entries
context: Using the app as a primary tool for habit tracking
Accessing the app across different sessions/devices
constraints: Technical failures disrupt the therapeutic process
Requires a stable environment to feel secure about their sensitive personal data

personas.name: Accessibility focused user
personas.description: A user with specific visual or physical needs who requires an inclusive and readable interface.
goals: Read interface content easily without eye strain
Access crisis support in a variety of accessible formats
pain_points: Inaccessible color schemes that are hard to read
Lack of inclusive options for deaf or hard-of-hearing users
context: Using the app while managing visual impairments or disabilities
Seeking support during periods of high sensory stress
constraints: UI must adhere to clear accessibility standards
Content must be inclusive of diverse user backgrounds

personas.name: Insight-driven tracker
personas.description: A self-aware user focused on long-term data analysis to understand their symptoms and emotional triggers.
goals: Recognize recurring emotional patterns over time
Understand the circumstances affecting their mood
pain_points: Difficulty evaluating feelings without structured guidance
Vague insights that lack actionable background information
context: Using the app for daily self-reflection and journaling
Reviewing bi-weekly reports to monitor progress
constraints: Requires clear data visualization and summaries
Needs high-quality reflection questions to trigger insights

personas.name: Clinical patient-collaborator
personas.description: A user who is actively in treatment and uses app data as a bridge to communicate with their therapist or doctor.
goals: Share accurate mood reports with health professionals
Identify specific topics to address during therapy sessions
pain_points: Forgetting symptoms or feelings between medical appointments
Lack of easy ways to export data for professional review
context: Regularly visiting a therapist, doctor, or mental health team
Using the app as a supplemental tool to professional clinical care
constraints: Data must be printable or easily sharable
Report formatting must be clear enough for professional use, but simple enough for patient to understand
